content: The First Amendment to the U.S. Constitution protects five fundamental rights: freedom of speech, freedom of the press, freedom of assembly, the right to petition the government, and the right to practice one's faith (freedom of religion).
raw_content: The First Amendment to the U.S. Constitution protects five fundamental rights: freedom of speech, freedom of the press, freedom of assembly, the right to petition the government, and the right to practice one's faith (freedom of religion).
